Exploratory Data Analysis_2

Author

SU Bo-Han

Published

March 3, 2026

Modified

March 4, 2026

1. Installing and launching required R packages

pacman::p_load(tidyverse, ggstatsplot,ggplot2, plotly, scales,corrplot)

2. Loading the data

data_cleaned <- read_csv("data/colombia_customers_processed.csv")

3. Correlation Analysis

options(scipen = 999)
plotcorrelation_clv <- function (a) {
ggscatterstats(data = data_cleaned,
                 x = total_tx_volume, y = customer_lifetime_value,
                 type = "nonparametic") + facet_wrap(vars(!!sym(a)))
}
plotcorrelation_clv ("occupation")
plotcorrelation_clv ("education_level")
plotcorrelation_clv ("marital_status")
plotcorrelation_clv ("household_size")
plotcorrelation_clv ("customer_segment")
plotcorrelation_clv ("feedback_sentiment")
plotcorrelation_clv ("age_group")
data_cleaned <- data_cleaned %>%
  mutate(clv_segment = factor(clv_segment, 
                              levels = c("Platinum", "Gold", "Silver", "Bronze")))

p1 <- ggplot(data = data_cleaned, 
             aes(y = total_tx_volume, x = age_group)) + 
  geom_boxplot(aes(fill = clv_segment)) + 
  scale_fill_brewer(palette = "YlGnBu") + 
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, vjust = 1, hjust = 1)) + 
  scale_y_log10(labels = comma)

p1 %>% 
  ggplotly() %>% 
  layout(boxmode = "group")
